tldr: Replace Bucket's hosted feature management with Vercel's open-source Flags SDK as the framework integration layer in Next.js or SvelteKit.
why_switch: Free, MIT-licensed, framework-native integration for Next.js/SvelteKit
Edge-compatible flag evaluation without extra latency
Provider-agnostic: swap providers later without rewriting calls
Cleaner server/edge ergonomics than a generic SDK
Decouples your app code from a single flag vendor
what_to_export: All flag keys and current values per environment
Any A/B experiment splits in use
Stale or archived flags to retire instead of migrate
Identity / context fields used for targeting
export_method: Use Bucket's dashboard or API (consult official Bucket docs for the supported export path) to pull every flag, its variations, and targeting logic. If no bulk export is available, script the REST API or transcribe flags manually. Capture identity attributes you currently target on so you can reproduce them in your Flags SDK identify() function.
import_method: Note that Flags SDK is a client/framework layer, not a flag store, so you still need a backing provider. Decide whether to use a supported adapter (LaunchDarkly, Statsig, Optimizely, Hypertune, ConfigCat, DevCycle, Flipt) or a custom static/edge-config provider. Define each flag with the flag() helper in your codebase, wire identify() to your auth context, and call the flag in server components, route handlers, or middleware. Consult the official Flags SDK docs at flags-sdk.dev for current adapter setup.

concept_mapping.note: Each flag is a typed JS export
Server-side or edge invocation in Next.js/SvelteKit
Returns the evaluation context per request
Choose LaunchDarkly, Statsig, Edge Config, etc.
Flags SDK does not ship its own dashboard

gotchas: Flags SDK is glue, not a backend; you must pick or build a provider
Best DX assumes Vercel hosting; other hosts work but lose some integrations
Limited value outside Next.js or SvelteKit
Some adapters can lag upstream provider features; verify parity before switching
Edge runtime restrictions may affect your custom adapter or identify() code

email.subject: Moving feature flags from Bucket to Vercel Flags SDK
email.body:
Hi team,

We're moving feature flags off Bucket and onto Vercel's open-source Flags SDK so flag calls are framework-native and we keep provider choice open. Plan: export every flag and its targeting rules from Bucket, pick a backing provider (or use Edge Config for static cases), then redeclare flags using the SDK's flag() helper and wire identify() to our auth context. We can roll over Next.js routes one at a time. Effort estimate: 4-12 hours.

Thanks
